label: Mild acne without abscess or medication red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild acne.
No abscess, cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular disease, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, pregnancy medication issue, systemic illness, or specialist-directed medication plan.
exclusion_criteria: Abscess, cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular acne, rapidly worsening swelling, systemic illness, or severe cutaneous adverse reaction.
Pregnancy medication decision, isotretinoin/teratogenic medication issue, immunocompromised host, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms are severe, scarring, worsening, or not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports acne framing, gentle skin care, avoiding picking/squeezing, clinician-directed medication use, and escalation for severe/worsening, scarring, or medication-related concerns. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild acne without abscess/cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular disease, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, pregnancy medication issue, systemic illness, immunocompromised/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed medication plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports acne framing, gentle skin care, avoiding picking/squeezing, clinician-directed medication use, and escalation for severe/worsening, scarring, or medication-related concerns. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild acne without abscess/cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular disease, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, pregnancy medication issue, systemic illness, immunocompromised/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed medication plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild acne
no abscess, cellulitis, severe acne, medication reaction, or systemic illness documented
no pregnancy medication, immunocompromised, pediatric, or specialist-directed dermatology pathway
